items.title: Johor Immigration removes counter numbers to curb counter-setting syndicates
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: An unconventional tactic by the Johor Immigration Department involving the removal of all counter number signs at southern border entry points has proven effective in reducing complaints of counter-setting misconduct involving its officers and personnel. Johor Immigration director Datuk Mohd Rusdi Mohd Darus said the measure was introduced after the department identified tactics used by syndicates to facilitate the movement of foreign nationals through a network of internal personnel lacking integrity. "At the designated counter, their illegal entry process would be handled by officers who had received substantial bribes.

items.title: Lorry driver detained over alleged use of forged permits to buy subsidised petrol in Terengganu
items.summary: BANDAR PERMAISURI, May 23 — A lorry driver has been detained on suspicion of using forged permits to purchase subsidised petrol for sale to industrial operators. Terengganu Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry (KPDN) director Mohd Mufsi Lat said the local man, in his 30s, was detained during an Ops Tiris 4.0 raid conducted at a petrol station in Merang near here at 7.30 am today. He said two drums containing 500 litres of subsidised petrol worth approximately RM1,600 were also seized during the operation.

items.title: Aidiladha getaway traffic crawls on Karak, PLUS highways as LLM warns of worse jams ahead
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 23 — Traffic was building up across several major highways by midday today as motorists began travelling ahead of the Hari Raya Aidiladha holidays, according to the Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM). According to Berita Harian, LLM senior assistant director of operations and maintenance Ahmad Kamil Abdul Halim said congestion was already forming along the Kuala Lumpur-Karak Expressway (KLK), particularly from the Middle Ring Road 2 (MRR2) to the Gombak toll plaza, with heavier traffic continuing towards Genting Sempah at KM30.5. LLM said 2.9 million vehicles used highways nationwide yesterday as holiday traffic began building up.

items.title: MACC seminar explores polygraph use in employment screening
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) yesterday organised the Malaysian Polygraph Seminar: The Role of Polygraph in Employment Screening at the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Academy (MACA), aimed at strengthening understanding of polygraph technology in integrity and security applications. In a statement, it said the seminar focused on enhancing knowledge of polygraph use as a support tool in employment screening, organisational integrity management and security-related investigations. The programme featured speakers from enforcement and academic institutions, including Assistant Superintendent Ahmad Shahrulazmi Mazni from the police's Special Branch, Assistant Superintendent Mohammad Firdaus Ashaari from the Narcotics Crime Investigation Department, MACC Polygraph team coordinator Norhakimin Brahim and Dr Salwa Zolkaful of Universiti Teknologi Mara (UiTM).

items.title: US jury clears Boeing in lawsuit over 737 MAX grounding losses
items.summary: NEW YORK: A US jury found Friday that aerospace giant Boeing was not liable for lost revenue in a lawsuit involving its 737 MAX jets, which were grounded for 20 months following two deadly crashes in 2018 and 2019. Polish airline LOT had accused Boeing of fraud and sued for US$250 million in lost income after the company's alleged "purposeful and negligent false representations and omissions concerning the 737 MAX aircraft," the initial complaint said. Last November, another jury awarded the widower of one of the MAX victims US$28.45 million.

items.title: Do not wait, Anwar tells agencies to reach small traders
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has directed micro-credit agencies to engage small traders and hawkers rather than wait for applicants to seek them out. Eight agencies set up booths and mobile services at the market to provide direct briefings to traders and visitors, namely Bank Simpanan Nasional (BSN), Amanah Ikhtiar Malaysia, National Entrepreneurial Group Economic Fund (Tekun Nasional), Agrobank, Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara), Bank Rakyat, the Credit Counselling and Debt Management Agency (AKPK) and the Companies Commission of Malaysia. The target groups include small traders, night market hawkers, women, youth, gig workers, TVET graduates, small contractors, padi farmers and asnaf group.

items.title: Thunderstorm warning issued nationwide, heavy rain until 4pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning involving several states nationwide, with heavy rain and strong winds expected until 4pm today. Several districts in Kelantan are also affected, namely Tumpat, Pasir Mas, Kota Bharu, Bachok and Pasir Puteh. In Terengganu, the areas under alert are Hulu Terengganu, Kuala Terengganu, Marang, Dungun and Kemaman.

items.title: Malaysian youths are judging governments by delivery, not drama, says minister
items.summary: KANGAR, May 23 — Malaysian youth today are becoming more mature and wiser in evaluating the effectiveness of a government based on its performance and the country’s achievements, said Youth and Sports Minister Dr Mohammed Taufiq Johari. He said the acceptance by the young generation of the current unity government has been positive, including among rural youth who are increasingly aware of current issues and national development. According to him, young people today no longer focus solely on political rhetoric, but instead assess the government based on economic performance, employment opportunities and national stability.

items.title: Anwar applauds Serdang Hospital team for world-class, life-saving cardiac care
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 23 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today congratulated the staff of Sultan Idris Shah Hospital (HSIS), particularly the Heart Centre team, for their outstanding achievements in delivering high-quality healthcare services to the public. In a Facebook post today, Anwar said the hospital’s ability to manage about 60 heart patients daily, including critical cases, reflected world-class expertise, efficiency and a high level of preparedness among the country’s healthcare personnel. Hospital Serdang was officially rebranded as HSIS on May 8, 2023.

items.title: Traffic slow moving on major highways and city roads
items.summary: In a traffic update issued by the authority's Traffic Management Centre, congestion was recorded along stretches of the North-South Expressway (PLUS) involving both northbound and southbound routes. Traffic was also slow after the Sungai Besi toll plaza heading towards the city centre. Several routes along the Damansara-Puchong Highway (LDP) were also experiencing congestion, including northbound stretches from Puchong Utama to Puchong Intan, Puchong Industri to Puchong Jaya, and Kelana Jaya to SS2.

items.title: Fowl play: 20kg python caught lurking inside Tuaran chicken coop
items.summary: TUARAN, May 23 — A 20-kilogram reticulated python was captured by the Malaysian Civil Defence Force (APM) Tuaran district after it was found coiled inside a chicken coop at Taman Jelita 2 early Friday morning. Tuaran District and Tamparuli Unit APM officer Nur Azierah Alang said the team received an emergency call from a member of the public at 6.31am before deploying personnel to the location. “Fearing for the safety of family members, the complainant immediately contacted the Tuaran District Operations Control Centre (PKOD) for assistance,” she said in a statement.

items.title: [UPDATED] Bersama eyes all 56 Johor seats in statewide push
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) is targeting to contest all 56 Johor state seats in the next state election. Former Tiram assemblyman and former Johor National Community Policy Council vice-chairman S. Gopalakrishnan, who recently joined Bersama, said the party was identifying suitable constituencies to contest in the state. Bersama Johor Protem Committee chairman Abd Hamid Ali said former PKR leaders and members at state and division levels, including himself as former Johor State Leadership Council member, had officially left PKR and joined Bersama effective today.

items.title: Freed Gaza flotilla activists claim rape, torture and abuse by Israeli authorities [WATCH]
items.summary: ROME: Activists released from Israeli custody after being detained on a flotilla trying to bring aid to Gaza were subjected to abuse, organisers said on Friday, with several hospitalised with injuries and at least 15 reporting sexual assaults, including rape. A legal source in Italy said prosecutors there were investigating possible crimes including kidnapping and sexual assault. "The allegations raised are false and entirely without factual basis," an Israeli prison service spokesperson said in a statement.

items.title: Prime Minister Anwar calls for outreach to hawkers and SMEs still unaware of government loan facilities
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 23 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has urged the relevant agencies to intensify ground efforts to provide explanations and assistance to small and medium traders who are still unaware of financing facilities and aid provided by the government. He said hawkers and small traders should continue to be given attention, as they number hundreds of thousands but still lack a strong platform to voice their views. Regarding his visit, Anwar said the feedback he received from hawkers and small traders was generally very positive, with most expressing gratitude for the opportunity to obtain micro-financing facilities provided by the government and its agencies.

items.title: Madani values transcend religion, says Loke
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: The Malaysia Madani concept embodies universal values of compassion, mutual respect and civility, and is not confined to Muslims or any particular religion, Transport Minister Anthony Loke said. Speaking at the National Wesak Conference, Loke said some communities still misunderstood Madani as being exclusively Islamic. Loke said the principles underpinning Malaysia Madani closely align with Buddhist teachings on compassion, kindness and helping others.

items.title: Rafizi-linked Bersama forms Johor pro-tem committee, eyes all 56 seats in state polls
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 22 — Johor has become the first state to set up a pro-tem committee for the Malaysian United Party (Bersama) nationwide ahead of the coming state election. He said Johor’s membership currently consists of about 30 per cent former PKR members, with the majority comprising veteran and founding members. “The Johor Bersama committee members consist of former PKR state and division leaders, including a former state assemblyman.

items.title: Pahang Sultan urges Cameron Highlands farmers to reduce pesticide use
items.summary: KUANTAN: Farmers and greenhouse operators in Cameron Highlands have been urged to adopt agricultural practices that minimise the use of pesticides and harmful chemicals. His Royal Highness said the Dutch government and companies from the Netherlands had shared expertise, technology and innovative farming practices with farmers in Cameron Highlands to improve the quality and productivity of local produce. Al-Sultan Abdullah said modern agricultural approaches, including smart farming technology and biological control methods, could help reduce dependence on pesticides and harmful chemicals.

items.title: Sabah maps out 25-year forest plan, pledges to retain 50pc forest cover
items.summary: Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said the state government, through the Sabah Forestry Department, is drafting a Statewide Forest Master Plan that will serve as a guideline for managing Sabah’s forest resources over the next 25 years. Hajiji said Sabah currently maintains 63 per cent forest cover, comprising 3.85 million hectares gazetted as forest reserves and other protected areas. At the same time, Hajiji said Sabah has identified about 400,000 hectares of degraded forest reserve land for forest plantation development under the Forest Plantation Development Action Plan 2022–2036.

items.title: Toyota Hilux driver allegedly high on ‘mushroom vape’ crashes into pedestrian and shopfront in Kuantan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 23 — A 21-year-old driver high on a suspected “mushroom vape” ploughed into an elderly pedestrian and smashed into a shopfront in Kuantan yesterday, police said. According to Utusan Malaysia , Kuantan police chief Assistant Commissioner Ashari Abu Samah said the suspect had been driving a Toyota Hilux from Jalan Teluk Sisek towards the city centre before losing control outside a hardware store. Police later searched the vehicle and reportedly found a plastic bottle containing synthetic MDMA.

items.title: MetMalaysia warns of severe weather in six states, including Kota Kinabalu and Johor Bahru
items.summary: In a statement today, MetMalaysia said the affected areas on the East Coast are Kuala Terengganu and Marang in Terengganu, as well as Kuantan, Bera, Pekan and Rompin in Pahang. In Johor, the affected areas are Tangkak, Segamat, Mersing, Kulai, Kota Tinggi and Johor Bahru. Meanwhile, in Sabah, the warning covers the Interior areas of Sipitang, Tenom, Beaufort, Keningau and Tambunan, as well as the West Coast areas of Papar, Putatan, Penampang, Kota Kinabalu, Tuaran and Kota Belud.

items.title: No mass exodus from PKR, says Youth chief after Rafizi and Nik Nazmi departures
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 23 — PKR’s Youth wing today dismissed talk of a mass exodus from the party following the departure of two of its senior leaders, insisting only a handful of members had left and that support among younger members remained strong. According to the New Straits Times , Angkatan Muda Keadilan (AMK) chief Muhammad Kamil Abdul Munim said the party had not seen any significant wave of resignations after former deputy president Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli and former vice-president Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad quit PKR on May 17. Muhammad Kamil said PKR remained resilient despite the high-profile exits, pointing to the party’s survival through previous political crises.

items.title: Semporna PKR chief quits party after 27 years, cites shift from reformist ideals
items.summary: SEMPORNA, May 23 — Semporna PKR chief Mohd Amin Abdul Mem has resigned from all party posts and quit PKR after 27 years, citing a shift away from the party’s original reformist principles. “PKR today has slightly changed its rhythm. Mohd Amin expressed gratitude to party leaders and grassroots members before formally announcing his resignation and departure from PKR.

items.title: Govt to launch skills programme for schoolgirls nationwide
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government will introduce skills development programmes for schoolgirls nationwide this year to strengthen economic empowerment among young women. Women, Family and Community Development Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri said the initiative aims to equip girls with practical and income-generating skills before they leave school, reducing long-term social issues through greater financial independence. Nancy said the ministry's KasihniTA programme continued to support single mothers through financial literacy, psychosocial support and legal assistance.

items.title: Jailed Vietnamese tycoon's Hermes handbags sell for over US$500k at auction
items.summary: HANOI: A pair of luxury Hermes handbags that once belonged to a jailed Vietnamese property tycoon sold at auction for more than US$500,000, state media reported, as the government seeks to recover funds linked to a US$27 billion fraud. A confiscated Hermes bag with white gemstones sold Thursday at the Ho Chi Minh City Asset Auction Service Centre for 11.6 billion Vietnamese dong (US$440,000), state media reported. A second Hermes bag sold for 2.5 billion dong (US$95,000).

items.title: Pahang tracks illegal redtail catfish farms as invasive species threaten rivers
items.summary: KUANTAN: The Pahang Fisheries Department is tracking individuals believed to be involved in farming invasive fish species, including the redtail catfish, which threaten native fish in the state's major rivers. Its director, Roslan Abu Hasan, said the Agriculture and Food Security Ministry views the growing presence of invasive fish species in the country seriously, and that regulatory amendments were necessary to prevent the extinction of native species and protect river biodiversity. He said without serious and sustained efforts, the redtail catfish could continue to multiply in the state's main rivers, eventually threatening native species, including freshwater prawns, which may become increasingly difficult to find.

items.title: PM Anwar urges Malaysians to unite, reject hate politics at Wesak Day conference
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 23 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today called on Malaysians to unite in common values and reject hate politics perpetuated by a small segment of the society. Anwar also called on Malaysians to reflect on Buddhist values such as sila (moral conduct), karuna (compassion) and panna (wisdom) and said the values also resonate with Islamic teachings. Transport Minister Anthony Loke and Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh were also present at the event.

items.title: [UPDATED] Only a handful will follow Rafizi, Nik Nazmi, says PKR Youth
items.summary: KOTA BARU: PKR Youth has dismissed claims that the party is weakening following the departure of two of its senior leaders, who have since taken over a new political party. Its chief, Muhammad Kamil Abdul Munim, said PKR remained strong and united despite the resignations of former deputy president Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli and former vice-president Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad on May 17. "PKR has gone through many episodes.

items.title: Indonesia eyes 150mill barrels of Russian crude
items.summary: JAKARTA: Indonesia is working on a special import scheme and regulatory framework to fulfil its plan to import 150 million barrels of crude from Russia this year, an official said, with data showing only one shipment has reached the country so far. Russia exports around 5 million barrels of crude per day, mostly to China and India, and allocating such a large volume to a new buyer would mean diverting oil from others, which could prove difficult, oil traders told Reuters. Indonesia, which imports about 1 million bpd, will seek extra supplies even if Russian crude materialises to bolster supply security, government officials have said.

items.title: Thai firms warn labour shortage, rising costs could hit farming and manufacturing sectors
items.summary: Authorities should fast-track work permit renewals, particularly for Cambodian labour, to prevent workers from losing their legal status, thereby avoiding disruptions to the manufacturing and agriculture sectors during a critical time for the Thai economy, said Wiboon Suphakarnpongkul, vice chairman of the Thai Chamber of Commerce, speaking on behalf of the Joint Standing Committee on Commerce, Industry and Banking. About 3.9 million migrant workers are in Thailand, but Cambodian numbers have plunged to around 194,000 from about 550,000, he told a briefing. Many Cambodian workers have returned home since a Thai-Cambodian border clash last year.

items.title: China set for latest space launch, with Hong Kong astronaut aboard
items.summary: BEIJING: A Hong Kong astronaut will join a Chinese space mission for the first time as part of a three-person crew launching on Sunday, as Beijing edges closer to its goal of landing people on the Moon. The Shenzhou-23 mission will blast off at 11.08pm (1508 GMT) on Sunday from the Jiuquan Satellite Launch Centre in northwestern China, carrying three astronauts to the space station, China Manned Space Agency (CMSA) spokesman Zhang Jingbo told reporters on Saturday. The team comprises Lai Ka-ying, hailed by state media as Hong Kong's first astronaut, Zhu Yangzhu and Zhang Zhiyuan, the spokesman said.

items.title: [UPDATED] Anwar calls for end to politics of hate and division
items.summary: Speaking at the National Wesak Conference, Anwar said the majority of Malaysians must take a stand and lead with conscience instead of allowing fear, hatred and racial rhetoric to dominate public discourse. He said vocal minorities were spreading fear, hatred and division while appearing to dictate national narratives despite not representing the views of most Malaysians. he said.

items.title: Rayer wins RM100k in defamation suit over social media videos
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The Sessions Court here awarded Jelutong member of parliament RSN Rayer RM100,000 in damages after allowing his defamation suit against a social media user over a series of videos published online in 2024. Rayer's lawyer K. Kumarathiraviam said the suit concerned several videos uploaded on social media platforms in September 2024. Rayer, who is also Penang Hindu Endowments Board (PHEB) chairman, claimed in his suit that the videos contained defamatory statements that tarnished his reputation as a lawyer, elected representative and DAP leader.

items.title: Government expands diesel subsidy scheme to jeeps and pickups nationwide from June 1
items.summary: KUNDASANG, May 23 — The government has agreed to enhance the mechanism for implementing diesel subsidy distribution under the Subsidised Diesel Control System (SKDS) by expanding eligibility to include jeeps and pickup trucks for the land goods transport sector nationwide, effective June 1. Beginning June 1, 2026, companies in the land goods transport sector nationwide that own jeeps and pickup trucks may apply to register under SKDS,” he told reporters here today. He encouraged companies in the public land transport, land goods transport and water public transport sectors that have not yet enjoyed petrol and diesel subsidies to register under the Subsidised Petrol Control System (SKPS) and SKDS.

items.title: Queen leaves for special visit to Turkiye
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia, left for a special visit to Turkiye today. According to a post on Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar's official Facebook page, Her Majesty was accompanied by the Tunku Temenggong of Johor, Tunku Idris Iskandar Al-Haj Ibni Sultan Ibrahim; Tunku Panglima of Johor, Tunku Abdul Rahman Al-Haj Ibni Sultan Ibrahim; and Tunku Putera of Johor, Tunku Abu Bakar Al-Haj Ibni Sultan Ibrahim. The private aircraft carrying the royal entourage departed from the Royal Hangar at Senai International Airport at 9am.

items.title: Suspects flee with Ford Ranger after test drive in Gemencheh
items.summary: District police chief Superintendent Amiruddien Sariman said the incident occurred about 5.50pm after the suspects arranged to meet the owner upon seeing an advertisement on social media. The stolen vehicle is a black Ford Ranger 2.0L Raptor bearing the registration number VEP 8787. He said the suspects arrived in a dark Mini Cooper Cross bearing the registration number VLQ 1366.

items.title: Warsh named Fed chairman as inflation climbs, consumer sentiment dives
items.summary: "We're going to get the rates down," Trump said later at Rockland Community College in Suffern, New York. I had a rotten head of the Fed, and now I have a great head of the Fed today, Kevin Warsh." "Our mandate at the Fed is to promote price stability and maximum employment," Warsh said.

items.title: Rich cartels challenge for US border agents
items.summary: US Customs and Border Protection (CBP) deputy agent in charge Rich White said the financial imbalance between Mexican cartels and border patrol operations remain one of the agency's biggest challenges. White said the CBP's Arizona sector operated on a budget of about US$24 million, with roughly US$22 million spent on keeping vehicles operational, including fuel, maintenance and tyres needed to patrol the vast region. Asked about cartel resources, White said: "Billions.

items.title: Facebook ‘buyers’ flee with Ford Ranger after test drive in Tampin, crash into SUV during getaway (VIDEO)
items.summary: Tampin police chief Superintendent Amiruddien Sariman said the pair arrived at about 5.50pm in a dark-coloured Mini Cooper Cross to inspect and test-drive the pickup truck. “The suspect contacted the complainant and said he wanted to buy the 4x4 advertised on Facebook,” Amiruddien said in a statement today. Amiruddien said the stolen vehicle was identified as a black Ford Ranger 2.0L Raptor, with estimated losses amounting to RM100,000.

items.title: Transparency, governance key for strategic GLC investments: Anwar
items.summary: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said losses alone are not the main concern if investments are critical for Malaysia's future economic development. Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan, Securities Commission Malaysia chairman Datuk Mohammad Faiz Azmi and Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir were present at the event. Anwar, who is also Finance Minister, said the government is taking major efforts to combat corruption and strengthen governance to address long‑standing structural issues.

items.title: [UPDATED] My loudest critics are politicians who have 'fallen off the tracks', says Anwar
items.summary: "When I ask the small traders, they say Alhamdulillah. Anwar was speaking to reporters during a visit to the Taman Melawati farmers' market here, where microcredit agencies had set up booths to engage directly with hawkers and small traders. Eight agencies set up booths and mobile services at the market to provide direct briefings to traders and visitors.

items.title: Anwar jokes politics can pause for ‘nasi dagang’ after swipe at ‘whining’ politicians
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 23 — Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today took a swipe at politicians who he said had lost sight of their original political struggle, describing them as louder complainers than the country’s small traders. According to Berita Harian , the prime minister said he admired the resilience of small-time traders, whom he described as complaining far less than big business players or certain politicians. “No politics today,” he said jokingly.

items.title: Govt expands diesel subsidy eligibility to jeeps, pickup trucks nationwide
items.summary: KUNDASANG: The government has agreed to enhance the implementation mechanism for diesel subsidy distribution under the Subsidised Diesel Control System (SKDS) by expanding eligibility to include jeeps and pickup trucks for the land goods transport sector nationwide, effective June 1. Beginning June 1, companies in the land goods transport sector nationwide that own jeeps and pickup trucks may apply to register under SKDS," he told reporters here today. He encouraged companies in the public land transport, land goods transport and water public transport sectors that have yet to receive petrol and diesel subsidies to register under the Subsidised Petrol Control System (SKPS) and SKDS.

items.title: Pakistan army chief in Teheran as Iran weighs US peace offer
items.summary: Teheran: Pakistan's army chief arrived in Teheran on Friday, as diplomacy to end the Middle East war gathered pace and Iran considered the latest peace proposal even as US media reported Washington was potentially considering new strikes on the Islamic republic. However, Pakistan remains the official mediator," said the Iranian foreign ministry's Baqaei. Iran's foreign minister Abbas Araghchi said Teheran was engaged in the diplomatic process despite what he called "repeated betrayals of diplomacy" by the US and "military aggression against Iran", according to Iranian news agency Tasnim.

items.title: Perlis MB says state assembly will be dissolved if PAS reps table no-confidence motion in June
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 23 — Perlis Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah said he would dissolve the state assembly if six PAS assemblymen table a vote of no confidence against him during the sitting scheduled from June 3 to 5, raising the prospect of fresh political turmoil within Perikatan Nasional (PN). He said the Perlis PN administration was formed after a change in the state government, adding that the Raja of Perlis had previously entrusted him with maintaining the coalition government following his appointment as menteri besar. “However, Perlis remains under the PN government,” he said.

items.title: China's Xi orders rescue efforts after Shanxi coal mine blast kills 8, traps 38
items.summary: BEIJING: Chinese President Xi Jinping has ordered all-out rescue efforts after a gas explosion at a coal mine in northern China's Shanxi province killed eight people and left 38 trapped underground, state media reported on Saturday. The gas explosion occurred late Friday at the Liushenyu coal mine in Qinyuan county, with 247 workers on duty underground, state media Xinhua reported. Xi called for authorities to "spare no effort" in treating the injured and conducting search and rescue operations, while ordering a thorough investigation into the cause of the accident and strict accountability in accordance with the law.

items.title: Hotel tissue raid in Kota Kinabalu lands 56‑year‑old with RM50 fine
items.summary: Yesterday, Patrick Chau pleaded guilty before Magistrate Dzul Elmy Yunus to the offence at 9.19am on May 10. The prosecution said Chau was seen leaving the hotel toilet area towards the parking area before being stopped. Inspector Mohamad Hamidi Mohamad Hamzah prosecuted.

items.title: Sarawak Teachers Union calls for continued 300‑litre RON95 subsidy to ease rural teachers’ travel burden
items.summary: KUCHING, May 23 — Sarawak Teachers Union (STU) has called on the government to consider maintaining the 300-litre monthly RON95 petrol subsidy specifically for educators in the state, particularly those serving in rural and interior areas. Its president Kullin Djayang said the proposal was based on several key factors affecting teachers’ welfare and rising cost of living in Sarawak. He added that many teachers also commute daily from home to school due to the shortage of teachers’ quarters in numerous schools across the state, further increasing fuel consumption.

items.title: Not perfect but I'm giving it my all for the nation, says PM [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim uploaded an emotional posting on his personal reflection on duty, sacrifice and leadership, accompanied by the evergreen ballad Bukan Kerana Nama by legendary rock singer Ramli Sarip. "We stand firmly behind the leadership of Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. Continue leading Malaysia towards greater glory," he wrote.

items.title: Strict quarantine clears 27,000 cattle from Thailand for Aidiladha, says quarantine department
items.summary: These livestock have undergone strict screening and quarantine procedures by the Malaysian Quarantine and Inspection Services Department (Maqis). “These two are among the main diseases we monitor throughout the quarantine,” he told Bernama at the Maqis Animal Quarantine Station in Padang Besar. He explained that some importers house their cattle in private quarantine premises approved by Maqis rather than at government stations, though Maqis officers regularly monitor the livestock.

items.title: Arthur Kurup leads symbolic planting of 1,000 trees in Sook for forest sustainability
items.summary: Minister of Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Datuk Seri Arthur Joseph Kurup said the Sook Lake Forest Reserve in Pensiangan was selected as the venue for the tree-planting programme held in conjunction with the National-level International Day of Forests 2026 celebration. He said the three-day programme, which runs until Sunday, is being carried out in collaboration with local leaders, corporate partners, and participation from Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan (SMK) Sook and SMK TrusMadi students. The Pensiangan member of Parliament said this year’s celebration, themed ‘Forests and Economies’, aims to highlight green resources as a highly valuable alternative economic source for communities, particularly in rural areas, whether through ecotourism, sustainable forest products or sustainable agriculture.

items.title: Jakim to harness AI in MyeHALAL 2.0, promises faster halal certification approvals
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 23 — The Department of Islamic Development Malaysia (Jakim) is targeting a shorter processing period for halal certification applications through the use of artificial intelligence (AI) technology. Its director-general, Datuk Dr Sirajuddin Suhaimee, said the move would be realised through the introduction of the MyeHALAL 2.0 system, supported by the MyHALALINGREDIENTS database, within the next few months. Apart from the use of AI, Sirajuddin said Jakim had also introduced a whitelist initiative for companies with a high level of commitment and strong compliance records to further expedite the halal certification approval process.

items.title: PM Anwar renews congratulations to Tamil Nadu CM Vijay in phone call, hints at September visit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 23 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim yesterday held a telephone conversation with Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay. In a Facebook post, Anwar said he once again congratulated Vijay on the significant responsibility he was undertaking in serving the people of Tamil Nadu. Anwar also prayed that Vijay would continue to be granted strength and wisdom to lead and administer effectively for the well-being of the people and the future of Tamil Nadu.

items.title: MACC to appeal High Court ruling dismissing bid to freeze RM548m Singapore funds linked to Na’imah and son
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 23 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) will file a notice of appeal against the Kuala Lumpur High Court’s decision today, which dismissed the prosecution’s application for an access restriction order against RM548 million in investment funds. On June 19, 2025, MACC, acting as the prosecuting authority, filed an ex parte application to restrain Na’imah, her son Muhammed Amir Zainuddin, two individuals, five companies and any related parties from dealing with assets outside Malaysia, namely investment funds of £21 million (about RM121 million) and US$99 million (about RM423 million) held in Singapore. The application was filed under Section 53 of AMLATFPUAA 2001, following investigations under Section 113 of the Income Tax Act and Section 4(1) of AMLATFPUAA 2001.

items.title: JPJ to unleash drones nationwide during Aidiladha ops, keeping watch on 288 accident hotspots
items.summary: DENGKIL, May 23 — The Road Transport Department (JPJ) will maximise the use of drones during Ops Hari Raya Aidil Adha (Ops HRAA), which will be carried out from May 24 to 31. JPJ deputy director-general (Planning and Operations) Datuk Jazmanie Shafawi said that six drones would be deployed at hotspot areas to enhance the detection of various traffic offences. Jazmanie said the use of drones was first introduced during the previous Hari Raya Aidilfitri operation, resulting in JPJ issuing 5,000 notices under Section 114 of the Road Transport Act 1987 (Act 333).

items.title: Prominent lawyer mourns son killed as Volkswagen collides with cement lorry on Karak Highway
items.summary: Datuk Akberdin Abdul Kader, 62, a prominent lawyer, said his son Adli Hakim Akberdin, 24, a student at the International Islamic University of Malaysia (IIUM), had planned to marry his close friend Athirah Najwa Mohd Tajul Ashikin, 27, who was also seriously injured in the incident. “The girl who was badly injured (Athirah Najwa) was supposed to be my son’s future wife, and we had already planned for them to get married next year,” he said when met at the Section 21 Muslim Cemetery here yesterday. Meanwhile, Akberdin said Adli Hakin, his fifth child, was a good-natured, obedient son who was well-liked by family members.

items.title: PM Anwar tells forum Malaysia must embrace risk in AI and digital ventures to secure future growth
items.summary: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said losses alone are not the main concern if the investments are critical for the country’s future economic development. Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan, Securities Commissioner Malaysia chairman Datuk Mohammad Faiz Azmi and Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir were present at the event. Anwar, who is also the Finance Minister, said the government is taking major efforts to combat corruption and strengthen governance to address long-standing structural issues.

items.title: PAS’ Tuan Ibrahim says Bersatu actions weakening Perikatan, warns against repeating history
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 23 — PAS deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man has publicly rebuked Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia over a series of recent actions he said had weakened Perikatan Nasional (PN) and disrupted the administration of several Opposition-led states. According to the New Straits Times , Tuan Ibrahim said political alliances must be anchored on trust and solidarity, warning that recent developments involving Bersatu were straining ties within the coalition ahead of the 16th general election. Tuan Ibrahim also appeared to caution Bersatu against escalating internal disciplinary actions that could further fracture cooperation within PN.

items.title: Home Ministry pushes MyNIISe rollout, touts safer, quicker immigration services at your fingertips
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 23 — Malaysians are encouraged to download the National Integrated Immigration System (MyNIISe) application and complete their registration to enjoy more efficient and seamless digital immigration services. Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the application was introduced to enable various key processes to be carried out directly via smartphones without the need to go through complicated procedures. “The MyNIISe application was introduced to provide a smoother, faster and more user-friendly travel and immigration experience,” he said in a Facebook post yesterday.

items.title: PAS issues directive barring unauthorised comments on party developments, urges members to remain calm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 23 — PAS has instructed its leaders and members not to comment publicly on recent developments involving the Islamist party without prior approval from its top leadership. Its secretary-general, Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan, said the directive applies across all media and communication platforms, including social media, to prevent confusion and misinterpretation. “PAS requests that all PAS leaders and members refrain from making any statements or comments regarding the latest developments involving PAS and other parties through any media or communication channels unless with special permission from the party,” he said in a statement yesterday.
